1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
BLKSEA_OMI_SEASTATE_extreme_var_swh_mean_and_anomaly 
IBI_OMI_SEASTATE_extreme_var_swh_mean_and_anomaly 
MEDSEA_OMI_SEASTATE_extreme_var_swh_mean_and_anomaly 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
The SEASTATE significant wave height extreme variability indicators are based on the 
computation of the 99th percentile - which represents approximately the highest 1% of 
significant wave height (SWH) values  - derived from model  outputs. This approach 
follows the methodology applied by Woodworth and Blackman (2004) for extreme 
sea level analysis and is used to detect changes in the frequency distribution of the 
variable of interest. 
 
All data used for the computation of the extre me variability indicators were obtained 
from the Copernicus Marine Environment Monitoring Service (CMEMS). The analysis is 
performed on SWH data for three CMEMS regions: the Black Sea (BLKSEA), Iberia -
Biscay-Ireland (IBI), and the Mediterranean Sea (MEDSEA ). For each region, the 
analysis combines information from both Multi -Year Products (MYP) and Near Real -
Time (NRT) Analysis Products. 
 
The following table summarizes the CMEMS products used for the analysis for each 
region. 
 
Black Sea 
(BLKSEA) 
MYP CMEMS product BLKSEA_MULTIYEAR_WAV_007_006 
PUM:

BLK-PUM-007-006.pdf

https://documentation.marine.copernicus.eu/QUID/CMEMS-
BLK-QUID-007-006.pdf
